dev.jeka.core.api.depmanagement

Class JkCoordinate

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.depmanagement.JkCoordinate

  • public final class JkCoordinate
extends java.lang.Object
    Represents a set of identifiers for binary artifacts.

    The coordinate pattern is expressed as follows:

    • group:name
    • group:name:version
    • group:name:classifier(s):version
    • group:name:classifier(s):type:version

    Notes:

    • Classifiers can be a single classifier or a comma-separated list (e.g., linux,mac).
      • . In this case, the coordinate stands for two artifacts.
    • The empty string represents the default classifier.
    • Version can be:
      • '?' if unspecified
      • '+' to select the highest available version

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class and Description
      static class  JkCoordinate.ConflictStrategy 
      static class  JkCoordinate.JkArtifactSpecification
      When declaring a module dependency, we implicitly request for the main artifact of this module.

      Creates a JkCoordinate to the specified getModuleId with unspecified version

    • Method Detail

      • of

        public static JkCoordinate of(JkModuleId jkModuleId)
        Creates a JkCoordinate to the specified getModuleId with unspecified version

      • of

        public static JkCoordinate of(JkModuleId jkModuleId,
                              JkVersion version)
        Creates a JkCoordinate to the specified getModuleId and JkVersion.

      • of

        public static JkCoordinate of(JkModuleId jkModuleId,
                              java.lang.String version)
        Creates a JkCoordinate of the specified moduleId and version range.

      • of

        public static JkCoordinate of(java.lang.String group,
                              java.lang.String name,
                              java.lang.String version)
        Creates a JkCoordinate to its group, name and version range. The version range can be any string accepted by JkVersion.of(String).

      • of

        public static JkCoordinate of(java.lang.String coordinate,
                              java.lang.Object... tokens)
        Creates a new JkCoordinate from the given string representation of a dependency coordinate. T
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the coordinate format does not adhere to the expected pattern.

      • isTransitiveByDefault

        public boolean isTransitiveByDefault()

      • isCoordinateDescription

        public static boolean isCoordinateDescription(java.lang.String candidate)
        Returns true if the specified candidate matches to a module description.

      • getModuleId

        public JkModuleId getModuleId()
        Returns the getModuleId of this dependency.

      • getVersion

        public JkVersion getVersion()
        Returns the version of the module this dependency is constrained to.

      • hasUnspecifiedVersion

        public boolean hasUnspecifiedVersion()
        Returns true if the version of the module for this dependency is not specified.

      • withVersion

        public JkCoordinate withVersion(JkVersion version)
        Returns a JkModuleDependency identical to this one but with the specified static version. If the specified version is null then returned version is this one.

      • withVersion

        public JkCoordinate withVersion(java.lang.String version)

      • withClassifiers

        public JkCoordinate withClassifiers(java.lang.String classifier)
        See Also:
        withClassifierAndType(String, String)

      • withClassifierAndType

        public JkCoordinate withClassifierAndType(java.lang.String classifiers,
                                          java.lang.String type)
        Returns a JkModuleDependency identical to this one but with the specified classifiers and type as the only JkCoordinate.JkArtifactSpecification for this dependency
        Parameters:
        classifiers - classifiers separated with ','. Example 'linux,max' stands for linux and mac classifier. ',mac' stands for the default classifier + mac classifier

      • withClassifier

        public JkCoordinate withClassifier(java.lang.String classifier)
        See Also:
        withClassifierAndType(String, String)
